name: Lawrence Lucie
abstract: Lawrence Lucie (December 18, 1907 – August 14, 2009) was an American jazz guitarist.

works.t: Call It Stormy Monday (But Tuesday Is Just as Bad)

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Ella Fitzgerald
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Louis Armstrong
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Count Basie
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Savoy Ballroom "The Home of Happy Feet"

works.t: When the Lights Go On Again (All Over the World)

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: There was Lawrence Lucie, guitar... as the rhythm section. ... I had seen Big Green with Fletcher Henderson. Dickie Wells and Bill Dillard I had worked with, but I did not know the others although Russell, Paque, Mills and Lucie were New York musicians.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I got the musicians, had Lawrence Lucie on guitar, Russ Andrews on tenor and Wynton Kelly on piano

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The rhythm section was Ernest Hill, Lawrence Lucie, guitar, Teddy Wilson and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Another one that followed him in later years that I think was at the Grand Terrace was Lawrence Lucie... But anyway, that was the rhythm section in Fletcher's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: someone we haven’t mentioned yet which is Lawrence Lucie, the rhythm guitarist. He actually goes by Larry but he knows that everyone knows it’s Lawrence Lucie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: I was with him for about six months and we needed a guitar and we brought in Lawrence Lucie... I was showing Lawrence Lucie how to do this and that.
